mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels in >50% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when either series declines independently, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals strengthening economic output and higher corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days exclusively during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during recovery phases, or the predictive power reverses to negative during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5% annually forces monetary authorities to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >55% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when payrolls rise but unemployment also rises, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal generates positive returns during early-cycle phases.
